finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:345; chapters/chapter_14.md:349; chapters/chapter_14.md:353; chapters/chapter_14.md:357; chapters/chapter_14.md:361; chapters/chapter_14.md:365
finding_results.notes: Roughened Doug's Detroit riff across six turns. Added visible self-correction at line 345 ('I think it's green. It might be teal, I don't — anyway'). Added over-specific dead-end detail at line 349 ('part of a Denny's sign, so I don't know what the chair is for'). Added false start at line 353 ('I stopped at — I think four rest stops'). Added thinking-out-loud texture at line 361 ('Other people's sleep, maybe. I don't know what that smells like, but it's in there'). Replaced four standalone aphoristic lines ('from a second location,' 'pauses with vending machines,' 'nobody is there on purpose,' 'nobody knows you're there') with flatter, more literal Doug-register phrasing that wanders before landing. Preserved the callback at line 457 ('Every room has that chair by the window'). The run now reads as Doug thinking out loud rather than delivering composed insights.

summary: Single finding addressed: roughened Doug's Detroit breakthrough riff (lines 341-365) so the monologue sounds discovered in real time rather than workshop-polished. Added one self-correction ('green/teal'), one false start ('I stopped at — I think four'), one over-specific dead-end detail ('part of a Denny's sign'), and one thinking-out-loud moment ('I don't know what that smells like, but it's in there'). Removed four detachable aphoristic constructions and replaced them with flatter, more literal phrasing consistent with Doug's style-bible register (flat, literal, only accidentally incisive). No other changes to the chapter.
